medical_history.history_of_present_illness: This is a ___ year old woman with a PMH significant for NASH cirrhosis (c/b grade I EV, EGD ___, and HE), who presents with ___ days of confusion, as well as a fall 2 days prior to admission (___). Ms. ___ was in her usual state of health until 2 days ago, when her daughter noticed she appeared more tired and less talkative. She had no complaints until 1 day ago when she complained of elbow pain. Per ___ also was complaining of some shortness of breath on the day of the fall.
medical_history.past_medical_history: NASH cirrhosis, complicated by grade I esophageal varices, hypertension, type II diabetes (diet controlled), hypothyroidism, history of subdural hematoma s/p fall, multi-drug resistant E. coli urinary tract infection.
medical_history.family_history: Sister - DM, Brother - ?stomach or liver cancer.

physical_examination.general_examination: Admission vitals: 98.3 | 176/74 | 68 | 18 | 95% RA. Thin, elderly woman in no acute distress. Tacky mucous membranes with white-yellow tongue coating. No scleral or sublingual icterus.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Very faint asterixis. Face symmetric. Moving all limbs against gravity. Not able to follow instructions. Cardiac: RRR, II/VI systolic ejection murmur heard at all auscultation points. Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation at 8 regions bilaterally.
